members.name: accept
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Accepts the function with the duration as a long milliseconds and int nanoseconds.
 *
 * @param <T> The function exception.
 * @param consumer Accepting function.
 * @param duration The duration to pick apart.
 * @throws T See the function signature.
 * @see StopWatch
 */

members.name: get
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets a Duration of unit stored in the system property at the given key.
 *
 * @param key  The property name.
 * @param unit The unit that the duration is measured in, not null.
 * @param def  The default value in the given unit.
 * @return a Duration of seconds.
 * @since 3.19.0
 */

tags.name: unit
tags.content: The unit that the duration is measured in, not null.

tags.name: def
tags.content: The default value in the given unit.

members.name: getMillis
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets a Duration of milliseconds stored in the system property at the given key.
 *
 * @param key The property name.
 * @param def The default value in milliseconds.
 * @return a Duration of milliseconds.
 * @since 3.19.0
 */

members.name: getNanosOfMiili
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets the nanosecond part of a Duration converted to milliseconds. *

* Handy when calling an API that takes a long of milliseconds and an int of nanoseconds. For example, * {@link Object#wait(long, int)} and {@link Thread#sleep(long, int)}. *

*

* Note that is this different from {@link Duration#getNano()} because a duration are seconds and nanoseconds. *

* * @param duration The duration to query. * @return nanoseconds between 0 and 999,999. * @deprecated Use {@link #getNanosOfMilli(Duration)}. */
members.code:
/** * Gets the nanosecond part of a Duration converted to milliseconds. *

* Handy when calling an API that takes a long of milliseconds and an int of nanoseconds. For example, * {@link Object#wait(long, int)} and {@link Thread#sleep(long, int)}. *

*

* Note that is this different from {@link Duration#getNano()} because a duration are seconds and nanoseconds. *

* * @param duration The duration to query. * @return nanoseconds between 0 and 999,999. * @deprecated Use {@link #getNanosOfMilli(Duration)}. */ @Deprecated public static int getNanosOfMiili(final Duration duration) { return getNanosOfMilli(duration); }

members.name: getNanosOfMilli
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets the nanosecond part of a Duration converted to milliseconds. *

* Handy when calling an API that takes a long of milliseconds and an int of nanoseconds. For example, * {@link Object#wait(long, int)} and {@link Thread#sleep(long, int)}. *

*

* Note that is this different from {@link Duration#getNano()} because a duration are seconds and nanoseconds. *

* * @param duration The duration to query. * @return nanoseconds between 0 and 999,999. * @since 3.13.0 */
members.code:
/** * Gets the nanosecond part of a Duration converted to milliseconds. *

* Handy when calling an API that takes a long of milliseconds and an int of nanoseconds. For example, * {@link Object#wait(long, int)} and {@link Thread#sleep(long, int)}. *

*

* Note that is this different from {@link Duration#getNano()} because a duration are seconds and nanoseconds. *

* * @param duration The duration to query. * @return nanoseconds between 0 and 999,999. * @since 3.13.0 */ public static int getNanosOfMilli(final Duration duration) { return zeroIfNull(duration).getNano() % 1_000_000; }

members.name: getSeconds
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets a Duration of seconds stored in the system property at the given key.
 *
 * @param key The property name.
 * @param def The default value in seconds.
 * @return a Duration of seconds.
 * @since 3.19.0
 */

members.name: isPositive
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Tests whether the given Duration is positive (duration > 0).
 *
 * @param duration the value to test
 * @return whether the given Duration is positive (duration > 0).
 */

members.name: of
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Runs the lambda and returns the duration of its execution.
 *
 * @param <E> The type of exception throw by the lambda.
 * @param consumer What to execute.
 * @return The Duration of execution.
 * @throws E thrown by the lambda.
 * @see StopWatch
 * @since 3.13.0
 */

tags.name: <E>
tags.content: The type of exception throw by the lambda.

members.name: of
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Runs the lambda and returns the duration of its execution.
 *

 * @param runnable What to execute.
 * @return The Duration of execution.
 * @throws E thrown by the lambda.
 * @see StopWatch
 * @since 3.13.0
 */

tags.name: <E>
tags.content: The type of exception throw by the lambda.

members.name: since
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Computes the Duration between a start instant and now.
 *
 * @param startInclusive the start instant, inclusive, not null.
 * @return a {@link Duration}, not null.
 * @since 3.13.0
 */

tags.name: startInclusive
tags.content: the start instant, inclusive, not null.

members.name: toChronoUnit
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Converts a {@link TimeUnit} to a {@link ChronoUnit}.
 *
 * @param timeUnit A non-null TimeUnit.
 * @return The corresponding ChronoUnit.
 */

members.name: toDuration
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Converts an amount and TimeUnit into a Duration.
 *
 * @param amount   the amount of the duration, measured in terms of the unit, positive or negative
 * @param timeUnit the unit that the duration is measured in, must have an exact duration, not null
 * @return a Duration.
 */

tags.name: amount
tags.content: the amount of the duration, measured in terms of the unit, positive or negative

tags.name: timeUnit
tags.content: the unit that the duration is measured in, must have an exact duration, not null

members.name: toMillisInt
members.javadoc:
/** * Converts a Duration to milliseconds bound to an int (instead of a long). *

* Handy for low-level APIs that take millisecond timeouts in ints rather than longs. *

*
    *
  • If the duration milliseconds are greater than {@link Integer#MAX_VALUE}, then return * {@link Integer#MAX_VALUE}.
    • *
  • If the duration milliseconds are lesser than {@link Integer#MIN_VALUE}, then return * {@link Integer#MIN_VALUE}.
    • *
* * @param duration The duration to convert, not null. * @return int milliseconds. */

tags.name: duration
tags.content: The duration to convert, not null.

members.name: zeroIfNull
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the given non-null value or {@link Duration#ZERO} if null.
 *
 * @param duration The duration to test.
 * @return The given duration or {@link Duration#ZERO}.
 */
